text.title: Continuous Discovery Cadence: Three Loops and Capacity Rules
text.summary: Three operating loops (daily, weekly, sprint) for integrating discovery into normal delivery rhythm, with capacity rules and leakage detection.

rule.statement: Run three loops in parallel: daily behaviour-watcher (analytics diffs, anomaly digests), weekly interview cycle with repository tagging, sprint-cadence opportunity-mapper updating the opportunity-solution tree.
rule.rationale: Each loop captures a different signal type at the right frequency. Collapsing all three into one weekly meeting misses fast behavioural signals and buries slow qualitative ones.

rule.statement: Daily loop output: route digests to humans only on threshold breaches, not on every metric movement. Continuous monitoring produces alert fatigue without threshold gates.
rule.rationale: Agents running 24/7 without threshold gates flood teams with noise, causing discovery fatigue that mirrors the problem it was meant to solve.

rule.statement: Allocate 15-20% of team capacity to discovery every sprint. Ship at least one experiment per sprint, even small ones. Cadence beats perfection.
rule.rationale: Discovery allocated to "leftover time" is the first thing cut when delivery pressure rises. Hard capacity allocation makes it visible in sprint planning.

rule.statement: Tag every interview snippet to an opportunity in a single repository. Without tagging, continuous discovery is continuous chatter. Maintain a rolling participant panel of 30-50 users with rotation to prevent sample bias.
rule.rationale: The same lesson "rediscovered" quarterly is a sign of a discovery practice with no memory. A tagged repository converts recurring insight cost into a one-time investment.

rule.statement: Combine at least two signal types per discovery theme: interview data alone, behavioural data alone, or support tickets alone are insufficient triangulation.
rule.rationale: Users say one thing and do another. Single-source discovery over-indexes on expressed rather than revealed preference.

section.title: Leakage Check

rule.statement: Every shipped feature must be traceable to a tagged opportunity in the research repository. Leakage (shipped features with no linked opportunity) above 30% signals delivery has decoupled from discovery.
rule.rationale: High leakage means the team is building based on internal opinion rather than discovered user need, negating the investment in continuous discovery.

section.reference: Python script that computes leakage ratio and exits non-zero above 30%.

rule.statement: Hold a monthly "what changed in user behaviour" review using analytics diffs, not anecdotes. Compare against trailing 4 weeks, not last 7 days, to filter seasonality.
rule.rationale: 7-day comparisons trigger false anomalies on weekends and holidays. Trailing 4-week baseline absorbs normal weekly patterns.
